body:
]> 18/07/2026 7:04 Bluebird skies and 32 lifts spinning!.

We're in for a sunny Saturday at Perisher with a top of 11 degrees and light winds up to 13km/h, making for another lovely day for bluebird laps!

It's currently a crisp -3 degrees, and last night's low humidity allowed 193 snow guns to fire up around the resort. Running since 5:30pm and still going at time of writing, the guns are topping up the base with man-made snow. Expect firm, fast conditions on the groomers today as a result.

A number of new lifts opened yesterday, including Brumby T-Bar, Pleasant Valley Quad Chair, Eyre T-Bar and the Perisher Quad Express to the very top, unlocking more terrain for skiers and boarders. That brings the total number of lifts scheduled to run today to 32. The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open terrain as quickly and safely as possible. Be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates.

Demo days are on at Perisher, with Atomic, Vandouml;lkl and Black Crows set up at Front Valley, showcasing all the latest gear for you to try!

Some of Perisher's best skiers and riders will take to the rails today to showcase their top tricks in the Perisher Parks Showdown. As a result, the lower section of the Slopestyle Terrain Park will be closed to the public from 9:00am - 4:00pm. Catch a glimpse of the action while on the V8 if you can!

Off the slopes, there's plenty of live music around the resort, including SAIJE performing on the deck at Blue Cow, and DJ Bolta bringing the vibes at Mid-Station. The Smith Street Band are also playing at The Station tonight, so grab your tickets if you haven't already.

As the sun goes down, the fun continues at Perisher with night skiing and boarding from 6:00pm to 8:30pm. Guests can ski and ride under the stars, capped off with a special fireworks display (weather dependent). NSW Fire and Rescue will also be down at the bottom of the V8, so be sure to pay them a visit if you're out and about this evening!

]]> weather-blank Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

]]> Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

]]> Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> 70.8 0 33 32 193 33 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
